title: Architecture
text: Datasets normal R data frames. Metadata carried attributes: keeps package compatible base R, dplyr, data.table, data-frame tooling still preserving submission metadata XPT report writers.
code: dm <- readRDS(extdata("dm.rds")) spec <- readRDS(extdata("sdtm-spec.rds")) dm <- apply_spec(dm, spec)  attr(dm, "label") #> [1] "Demographics" attr(dm$USUBJID, "label") #> [1] "Unique Subject Identifier" attr(dm$USUBJID, "sas.length") #> [1] 11 attr(dm$USUBJID, "xpt_type") #> NULL

title: Architecture
text: as_herald_spec() compact constructor dataset variable metadata. herald_spec() richer constructor used Define-XML.
code: simple_spec <- as_herald_spec(   ds_spec = data.frame(     dataset = "DM",     label = "Demographics",     class = "SPECIAL PURPOSE",     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ),   var_spec = data.frame(     dataset = "DM",     variable = c("STUDYID", "USUBJID"),     label = c("Study Identifier", "Unique Subject Identifier"),     type = c("text", "text"),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) )  simple_spec #> <herald_spec> #>   1 dataset, 2 variables

title: Architecture
text: Compiled rules live inst/rules. rule_catalog() flattens compiled corpus browsing, filtering, coverage checks. supported_standards() summarizes predicate coverage standard authority.

title: Architecture
text: Package errors inherit herald_error. Specific subclasses distinguish input, file, spec, rule, validation, Define-XML, report, runtime failures. lets pipelines catch broadly tests pin precise classes.
code: tryCatch(   validate(quiet = TRUE),   herald_error = function(e) class(e) ) #> [1] "herald_error_input" "herald_error"       "rlang_error"        #> [4] "error"              "condition"

title: Best Practices for Validation Projects
text: Keep submitted data, metadata, validation outputs, project setup separate. validation program read data/, metadata/, dictionaries/, write qc/. makes reruns predictable.
code: study/   data/     sdtm/       dm.xpt       ae.xpt     adam/       adsl.xpt       adae.xpt   metadata/     define.xml     spec.rds   dictionaries/     meddra/     whodrug/   programs/     validate-sdtm.R     validate-adam.R   qc/     sdtm-report.html     sdtm-report.xlsx     sdtm-report.json

title: Best Practices for Validation Projects
text: Stamp metadata writing transport files validation. keeps labels, lengths, expected types synchronized across XPT, Dataset-JSON, Define-XML, reports.
code: dm <- readRDS(extdata("dm.rds")) spec <- readRDS(extdata("sdtm-spec.rds"))  dm <- apply_spec(dm, spec)  attr(dm, "label") #> [1] "Demographics" attr(dm$USUBJID, "label") #> [1] "Unique Subject Identifier" attr(dm$USUBJID, "sas.length") #> [1] 11

title: Best Practices for Validation Projects
text: production, validate files plan submit, earlier -memory object. good pattern : Stamp metadata. Write XPT Dataset-JSON. Read written files back. Validate reread files. Write report formats.
code: root <- file.path(tempdir(), "herald-best-practices") data_dir <- file.path(root, "data", "sdtm") qc_dir <- file.path(root, "qc") dir.create(data_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE) dir.create(qc_dir, recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)  write_xpt(dm, file.path(data_dir, "dm.xpt"))  result <- validate(   path  = data_dir,   rules = character(0),   quiet = TRUE )  result$datasets_checked #> [1] "DM"

title: Best Practices for Validation Projects
text: let production programs hide rule policy. Define rule filters near top program. Use object validation call:
code: catalog <- rule_catalog()  rule_policy <- list(   standards   = c("SDTM-IG", "Define-XML"),   authorities = c("CDISC", "FDA"),   severity_map = c("Medium" = "High") )  head(catalog[catalog$standard %in% rule_policy$standards,              c("rule_id", "standard", "authority", "severity")]) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   rule_id    standard   authority severity #>   <chr>      <chr>      <chr>     <chr>    #> 1 DEFINE-001 Define-XML CDISC     Medium   #> 2 DEFINE-002 Define-XML CDISC     Medium   #> 3 DEFINE-003 Define-XML CDISC     Medium   #> 4 DEFINE-004 Define-XML CDISC     Medium   #> 5 DEFINE-005 Define-XML CDISC     Medium   #> 6 DEFINE-006 Define-XML CDISC     Medium policy_result <- validate(   path = data_dir,   standards = rule_policy$standards,   authorities = rule_policy$authorities,   severity_map = rule_policy$severity_map,   quiet = TRUE )  policy_result$rules_total #> [1] 965

title: Best Practices for Validation Projects
text: Pass dictionary providers directly validate() want run self-contained. Use session registration interactive work. licensed dictionaries MedDRA WHODrug, store source version project metadata construct providers one setup block.
code: dictionaries <- list(   "ct-sdtm" = ct_provider("sdtm") )  dict_result <- validate(   path = data_dir,   rules = character(0),   dictionaries = dictionaries,   quiet = TRUE )  dict_result$skipped_refs #> $datasets #> list() #>  #> $dictionaries #> list() dictionaries <- list(   "ct-sdtm" = ct_provider("sdtm", version = "bundled"),   "meddra"  = meddra_provider("dictionaries/meddra", version = "27.0"),   "whodrug" = whodrug_provider("dictionaries/whodrug", version = "2024-03") )

title: Best Practices for Validation Projects
text: Write three report formats herald_result. Use format different audience:
code: html <- file.path(qc_dir, "sdtm-report.html") xlsx <- file.path(qc_dir, "sdtm-report.xlsx") json <- file.path(qc_dir, "sdtm-report.json")  report(result, html) report(result, xlsx) report(result, json)  file.exists(c(html, xlsx, json)) #> [1] TRUE TRUE TRUE

title: Best Practices for Validation Projects
text: study usually multiple packages: SDTM, ADaM, sometimes standalone Define-XML checks. Use small metadata table loop .
code: jobs <- data.frame(   package = c("sdtm", "adam"),   path    = c("data/sdtm", "data/adam"),   html    = c("qc/sdtm-report.html", "qc/adam-report.html"),   xlsx    = c("qc/sdtm-report.xlsx", "qc/adam-report.xlsx"),   json    = c("qc/sdtm-report.json", "qc/adam-report.json"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  for (i in seq_len(nrow(jobs))) {   job <- jobs[i, ]   res <- validate(path = job$path, quiet = TRUE)   report(res, job$html)   report(res, job$xlsx)   report(res, job$json) }

title: Best Practices for Validation Projects
text: simple CI policy can fail high-impact fired findings still saving full report set review. CI, write reports first, stop:
code: is_blocking <- function(result) {   f <- result$findings   if (!nrow(f)) return(FALSE)   any(     f$status == "fired" &       f$severity %in% c("Reject", "Error", "High"),     na.rm = TRUE   ) }  is_blocking(result) #> [1] FALSE report(result, "qc/report.html") report(result, "qc/report.xlsx") report(result, "qc/report.json")  if (is_blocking(result)) {   stop("Blocking validation findings found. See qc/report.html.") }

title: herald cheatsheet
text: Pure-R CDISC conformance validation. Read submission datasets, attach metadata, run rules, ship reviewer-ready reports – without Java SAS.

title: herald cheatsheet
text: Round-trip XPT labels, lengths, types preserved. Tip: XPT label = 40 char max, variable label = 200; truncation throws herald_error_input.
code: dm <- read_xpt("dm.xpt") write_xpt(dm, "dm.xpt", label = "Demographics")

title: herald cheatsheet
text: CDISC’s transport replacement – preserves types ISO 8601 dates. Carries name, label, itemOID, standard every column.
code: ae <- read_json("ae.json") write_json(ae, "ae.json",   dataset_label = "Adverse Events",   iso_dates     = TRUE )

title: herald cheatsheet
text: Arrow-backed columnar storage. Best ADaM scale. Requires arrow; falls back clear herald_error_runtime absent.
code: adsl <- read_parquet("adsl.parquet") write_parquet(adsl, "adsl.parquet")  convert_dataset("dm.xpt", "dm.parquet")

title: herald cheatsheet
text: Round-trip Define-XML 2.1 document herald_spec. Carries codelists, methods, comments, value-level metadata, ARM display sections.
code: def  <- read_define_xml("define.xml") spec <- as_herald_spec(def)  is_herald_spec(spec) # TRUE

title: herald cheatsheet
text: Programmatic construction tests, fixtures, sponsor extensions.
code: spec <- herald_spec(   datasets  = list(     DM = list(label = "Demographics",               class = "SPECIAL PURPOSE")   ),   variables = list(     DM = list(       USUBJID = list(label = "Subject ID",                      length = 30, type = "text"),       AGE     = list(label = "Age", type = "integer")     )   ) )

title: herald cheatsheet
text: Print, summarise, validate schema. validate_spec() runs DEFINE-NNN rule subset – datasets needed.
code: print(spec) summary(spec) validate_spec(spec)   # define-xml structure rules  names(spec$datasets) spec$variables$DM$AGE

title: herald cheatsheet
text: Idempotent. Re-stamping replaces – never appends.
code: dm <- apply_spec(dm, spec)  attr(dm, "label")             # "Demographics" attr(dm$AGE, "label")         # "Age" attr(dm$AGE, "herald_length") # 8 class(dm)                     # data.frame + sdtm class

title: herald cheatsheet
text: Rules expecting herald_length, herald_type, dataset class fail-fast without stamping.
code: files <- list(DM = dm, AE = ae, EX = ex, VS = vs) files <- Map(apply_spec, files, list(spec))  # or pass to validate() and let it stamp validate(files = files, spec = spec)

code: mh   <- meddra_provider(path = "meddra/") who  <- whodrug_provider(path = "whodrug/") srs  <- srs_provider(release = "latest")  mh$contains("LLT", "Headache") who$lookup("ATC", "N02BE01")

text: providers share contains() + optional lookup() – rules don’t care source.

text: Rule IDs stable – safe pin study-level QC scripts.

title: Herald Cookbook
text: Use rule_catalog() see available, filter column. Pass chosen rule_id values validate(rules = ...).

text: read_define_xml() returns herald_define object. object exposes data-frame slots used validation Define-XML dependent rules.
code: define <- read_define_xml(xml_path)  define #> <herald_define> #>   2 datasets, 6 variables, 0 key-var mappings define$ds_spec[, c("dataset", "label")] #>   dataset          label #> 1      DM   Demographics #> 2      AE Adverse Events define$var_spec[, c("dataset", "variable", "label")] #>               dataset variable                     label #> IT.DM.STUDYID      DM  STUDYID          Study Identifier #> IT.DM.USUBJID      DM  USUBJID Unique Subject Identifier #> IT.DM.SEX          DM      SEX                       Sex #> IT.AE.STUDYID      AE  STUDYID          Study Identifier #> IT.AE.USUBJID      AE  USUBJID Unique Subject Identifier #> IT.AE.AETERM       AE   AETERM             Reported Term

title: Define-XML 2.1
text: write_define_html() renders reviewer-friendly HTML page spec.
code: html_path <- file.path(define_dir, "define.html") write_define_html(spec, html_path, define_xml = xml_path)  file.exists(html_path) #> [1] TRUE

title: Define-XML 2.1
text: Pass parsed Define-XML object validate() rule execution needs metadata present datasets alone.
code: dm <- readRDS(extdata("dm.rds")) dm <- apply_spec(dm, spec)  result <- validate(   files  = list(dm, define = define),   define = define,   rules  = character(0),   quiet  = TRUE )  result$datasets_checked #> [1] "DM"                        "Define_Codelist_Metadata"  #> [3] "Define_Dataset_Metadata"   "Define_MethodDef_Metadata" #> [5] "Define_Study_Metadata"     "Define_Variable_Metadata"

title: Define-XML 2.1
text: Keep Define-XML authoring pipeline dataset writing: Build read herald_spec. Apply spec datasets. Write XPT Dataset-JSON. Write Define-XML HTML. Read written Define-XML back. Validate submitted files parsed Define-XML object. catches drift metadata source actual submission artifacts reviewers see .

title: Controlled Terminology and Dictionary Providers
text: Register providers globally R session, pass directly validate(dictionaries = ...) one run. One-run registration avoids session state:
code: register_dictionary("ct-sdtm", ct_provider("sdtm")) list_dictionaries() #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   name    version    source  license   size_rows #>   <chr>   <chr>      <chr>   <chr>         <int> #> 1 ct-sdtm 2026-03-27 bundled CC-BY-4.0     45612 unregister_dictionary("ct-sdtm") dm <- data.frame(USUBJID = "S1", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)  result <- validate(   files = dm,   rules = character(0),   dictionaries = list("ct-sdtm" = ct_provider("sdtm")),   quiet = TRUE )  result #> ── herald validation -- Spec Checks Only ────────────────────────────── #> Rules: 0/0 applied #> Datasets checked: 1 #> Findings: 0 #> Duration: 0.02690029 secs

title: Extending herald
text: Every provider object (class herald_dict_provider) carries: name, version, source, license – metadata fields. size_rows – number rows underlying table. fields – column / field names can query. contains(value, field, ignore_case) – function returning logical vector parallel value. Required. info() – closure returns metadata . lookup(value, field) – optional. Returns matching rows richer rule diagnostics. Use new_dict_provider() construct one. validates contract sets S3 class.

title: Extending herald

code: allowed_units <- c("KG", "LB", "G", "MG")  unit_provider <- new_dict_provider(   name      = "study-units",   version   = "2026-04",   source    = "sponsor",   fields    = "code",   size_rows = length(allowed_units),   contains  = function(value, field = "code", ignore_case = FALSE) {     if (isTRUE(ignore_case)) {       toupper(value) %in% toupper(allowed_units)     } else {       value %in% allowed_units     }   } )  unit_provider$contains(c("KG", "kg", "stones")) #> [1]  TRUE FALSE FALSE unit_provider$contains(c("KG", "kg", "stones"), ignore_case = TRUE) #> [1]  TRUE  TRUE FALSE unit_provider #> <herald_dict_provider> #>   name     : study-units #>   version  : 2026-04 #>   source   : sponsor #>   license  : unknown #>   size     : 4 rows #>   fields   : code

what: Recipe: a tibble-backed provider with lookup()
title: Extending herald
text: sponsor codelists richer flat vector. Pass data.frame custom_provider() common case (returns full herald_dict_provider), hand-build new_dict_provider() add lookup() function.
code: sites <- data.frame(   code = c("S01", "S02", "S03"),   name = c("Boston General", "Madrid Clinic", "Tokyo Medical"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE )  site_provider <- new_dict_provider(   name      = "site-codes",   version   = "study-A-2026",   source    = "sponsor",   fields    = c("code", "name"),   size_rows = nrow(sites),   contains  = function(value, field = "code", ignore_case = FALSE) {     col <- sites[[field]]     if (isTRUE(ignore_case)) {       toupper(value) %in% toupper(col)     } else {       value %in% col     }   },   lookup = function(value, field = "code") {     sites[sites[[field]] %in% value, , drop = FALSE]   } )  site_provider$contains(c("S01", "S99")) #> [1]  TRUE FALSE site_provider$lookup("S02") #>   code          name #> 2  S02 Madrid Clinic

title: Extending herald
text: operator small function returns logical vector saying rows dataset fail check. Rules YAML catalog reference operators name supply arguments. Examples built-operators include iso8601, unique_key, subset_of, regex_match.

title: Extending herald
text: Every operator function signature: Length contract: returned vector must nrow(data) long unless data empty (return logical(0)).
code: op_<name> <- function(data, ctx, ...) {   # data : the data frame currently under check   # ctx  : run context (datasets, dict, missing_refs, ...)   # ...  : rule-supplied arguments (column names, regex, etc.)   #   # return: logical vector of length nrow(data)   #   TRUE  -- record fires (rule fails on this row)   #   FALSE -- record passes   #   NA    -- indeterminate / advisory }

title: Extending herald
text: pattern new op file R/ops-*.R looks like. actual registration uses internal helper invoked package load; function body part write. Key points: Return values: TRUE = fires, FALSE = passes, NA = advisory. Always vectorised nrow(data). scalar early exits. Return NA missing inputs; let engine route advisory / skipped channels. Argument names (column, etc.) exactly rule YAML passes via operator’s arg_schema.
code: op_positive <- function(data, ctx, column = NULL, ...) {   if (is.null(column)) {     return(rep(NA, nrow(data)))   }   x <- data[[column]]   if (is.null(x)) {     return(rep(NA, nrow(data)))   }   # FALSE = passes the check (positive); TRUE = fires (non-positive)   is.na(x) | !(is.numeric(x) & x > 0) }

title: Extending herald
text: rules need second dataset (e.g. “every USUBJID AE must exist DM”). Resolve reference run context: reference dataset absent, herald automatically records miss ctx$missing_refs$datasets. user sees result$skipped_refs hint provide dataset.
code: op_subject_in_dm <- function(data, ctx, ...) {   ref <- .ref_ds(ctx, "DM")     # internal helper, returns NULL if missing   if (is.null(ref)) {     return(rep(NA, nrow(data))) # engine logs missing ref + skips rule   }   !(data$USUBJID %in% ref$USUBJID) }

what: Why does my validate() return 0 rules?
title: Frequently Asked Questions
text: Three common causes: Empty rule filter. rules = character(0) deliberate smoke-test mode exercises result object without running anything. Drop argument run full corpus. Filter excludes every rule. Combining standards =, authorities =, rules = ANDs conditions. Inspect rule_catalog() first. Datasets match rule scope. rules dataset-scoped (e.g. AE-rules). pass DM, AE-rules silently scope.
code: # Smoke-test mode: rules_total = 0 by design r <- validate(files = dm, rules = character(0), quiet = TRUE) r$rules_total #> [1] 0  # Run the full corpus r <- validate(files = list(DM = apply_spec(dm, sdtm_spec)), quiet = TRUE) r$rules_total #> [1] 1762

title: Frequently Asked Questions
text: read_define_xml() validates Define-XML 2.1 schema herald conformance ruleset. Common rejections: Missing namespace declaration root element. title: What CDISC rules does herald check?
text: SDTM-IG (740 rules) Structural content checks across SDTM observation classes – domain existence, variable naming, controlled-terminology values, timing variables, relationships, supplemental qualifiers. Rules sourced CDISC SDTM SDTMIG Conformance Rules v2.0 (CDISC authority) FDA Technical Conformance Guide submissions guidance (FDA authority). ADaM-IG (790 rules) Dataset-level checks ADSL, BDS, OCCDS, ADTTE subject-level analysis datasets. Variable suffixes, baseline/change traceability, analysis flags, treatment period variables. Rules sourced CDISC ADaM Conformance Rules v5.0. Define-XML (231 rules) Structural conformance define.xml v2.1 files – OID uniqueness, codelist references, value-level metadata, origin types, ARM display metadata. CDISC-published rules plus herald gap-fill rules (HERALD authority) checks specification mandates neither CDISC Library FDA publishes machine-executable predicates. SEND-IG (1 rules) Initial SEND support; expanded coverage planned future release.

title: What CDISC rules does herald check?
text: rules herald executable predicate – operator tree fires dataset rows emits finding condition violated. small number narrative rules describe conformance requirement text whose predicate yet ported herald operator. Narrative rules never emit false findings; simply skipped.
code: cat <- rule_catalog() narrative <- cat[!cat$has_predicate, ] if (nrow(narrative) == 0L) {   message("All ", nrow(cat), " rules have executable predicates.") } else {   knitr::kable(     aggregate(rule_id ~ standard, data = narrative, FUN = length),     col.names = c("Standard", "Narrative rules (no predicate)")   ) }

title: What CDISC rules does herald check?
text: areas represent categories coverage thinner rule authoring actively progress: SEND-IG – 1 rule compiled today; SEND datasets pass structural xpt checks full conformance coverage yet available. SDTM-IG narrative rules – CDISC conformance XLSX contains rules whose predicates require complex multi-dataset joins (e.g., sponsor-defined key relationships, sequence variable cross-domain checks). ported incrementally. ADaM-IG narrative rules – similar SDTM, rules involving join-time derivation logic (baseline window checks, treatment-period variable matching) active predicate authoring. Progress tracked internally. 2026-04 corpus reflects 99.8% executable coverage across 1865 conformance rules.

code: catalog <- rule_catalog()  table(catalog$standard, catalog$authority) #>               #>               CDISC FDA HERALD #>   ADaM-IG       790   0      0 #>   Define-XML    225   0      6 #>   herald-spec     0   0    103 #>   SDTM-IG       659  81      0 #>   SEND-IG         1   0      0 head(catalog[catalog$has_predicate, c("rule_id", "standard", "severity", "message")]) #> # A tibble: 6 × 4 #>   rule_id standard severity message                                     #>   <chr>   <chr>    <chr>    <chr>                                       #> 1 1       ADaM-IG  Medium   ADSL dataset does not exist                 #> 2 10      ADaM-IG  Medium   A variable with a suffix of FL is equal to… #> 3 102     ADaM-IG  Medium   For every unique xx value of APERIOD, ther… #> 4 103     ADaM-IG  Medium   For every unique xx value of APERIOD, ther… #> 5 104     ADaM-IG  Medium   For every unique xx value of APERIOD, ther… #> 6 105     ADaM-IG  Medium   There is more than one value of APERIODC f…  rule_ids <- head(catalog$rule_id[catalog$has_predicate], 5)  r2 <- validate(   files = dm,   rules = rule_ids,   quiet = TRUE )  r2$rules_total #> [1] 5 r2$rules_applied #> [1] 1 supported_standards() #> # A tibble: 7 × 6 #>   standard    authority n_rules n_predicate n_narrative pct_predicate #>   <chr>       <chr>       <int>       <int>       <int>         <dbl> #> 1 ADaM-IG     CDISC         790         790           0         1     #> 2 Define-XML  CDISC         225         225           0         1     #> 3 Define-XML  HERALD          6           5           1         0.833 #> 4 herald-spec HERALD        103         100           3         0.971 #> 5 SDTM-IG     CDISC         659         659           0         1     #> 6 SDTM-IG     FDA            81          81           0         1     #> 7 SEND-IG     CDISC           1           1           0         1

title: Validation and Reporting
text: Findings stored tibble. useful columns triage rule_id, severity, status, dataset, variable, row, message. Filter base R preferred data-frame toolkit:
code: names(r2$findings) #>  [1] "rule_id"           "authority"         "standard"          #>  [4] "severity"          "severity_override" "status"            #>  [7] "dataset"           "variable"          "row"               #> [10] "value"             "expected"          "message"           #> [13] "source_url"        "p21_id_equivalent" "license"  if (nrow(r2$findings)) {   r2$findings[, c("rule_id", "severity", "status", "dataset", "variable", "row", "message")] } else {   "No findings emitted by the selected rules." } #> # A tibble: 1 × 7 #>   rule_id severity status dataset      variable   row message           #>   <chr>   <chr>    <chr>  <chr>        <chr>    <int> <chr>             #> 1 1       Medium   fired  <submission> NA          NA ADSL dataset doe… fired <- r2$findings[r2$findings$status == "fired", , drop = FALSE] high  <- r2$findings[r2$findings$severity %in% c("Reject", "High", "Error"), , drop = FALSE]  nrow(fired) #> [1] 1 nrow(high) #> [1] 0

title: Validation and Reporting
text: Sponsors often need study-specific severity policies. severity_map can override exact rule ID, regular expression, severity category.
code: r3 <- validate(   files = dm,   rules = rule_ids,   severity_map = c("Medium" = "High"),   quiet = TRUE )  if (nrow(r3$findings)) {   unique(r3$findings[, c("rule_id", "severity", "severity_override")]) } else {   "No emitted findings to override in this small run." } #> # A tibble: 1 × 3 #>   rule_id severity severity_override #>   <chr>   <chr>    <chr>             #> 1 1       High     Medium

title: Validation and Reporting
text: Use report() want extension-based dispatch. Use specific writers want explicit output.
code: report_dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "herald-reports") dir.create(report_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)  html <- file.path(report_dir, "report.html") xlsx <- file.path(report_dir, "report.xlsx") json <- file.path(report_dir, "report.json")  write_report_html(r2, html) write_report_xlsx(r2, xlsx) write_report_json(r2, json)  file.info(c(html, xlsx, json))[, c("size", "mtime")] #>                                             size               mtime #> /tmp/RtmpyJ2hCz/herald-reports/report.html 19484 2026-04-27 09:23:52 #> /tmp/RtmpyJ2hCz/herald-reports/report.xlsx  9561 2026-04-27 09:23:52 #> /tmp/RtmpyJ2hCz/herald-reports/report.json  2944 2026-04-27 09:23:52

title: herald - CDISC Validation in R
text: Pure-R conformance validation CDISC clinical data submissions. Reads SDTM ADaM datasets XPT, Dataset-JSON, Parquet files, checks published CDISC Library rules, controlled terminology, Define-XML 2.1 specs. Java runtime required. herald pure-R package teams need reproducible submission checks without Java desktop validator. reads clinical datasets, stamps metadata submission specification, runs SDTM, ADaM, Define-XML rules, writes review-ready reports. Status: pre-alpha. APIs can still change rule engine Define-XML round-trip completed.

title: herald - CDISC Validation in R
text: Clinical submission validation usually crosses several tools: SAS transport writers, spreadsheet specs, Define-XML tooling, controlled terminology lookups, Java validators, hand-built reports. herald puts core workflow one auditable R pipeline.

title: Pilot ADAE (Adverse Events Analysis Dataset, OCCDS) — adae
text: ADAE dataset CDISC ADaM pilot submission containing 254 adverse-event records across 50 pilot subjects. ADAE OCCDS (Occurrence Data Structure) following ADaM IG: row one occurrence adverse event, joined ADSL USUBJID carrying full MedDRA hierarchy plus serious-criteria, severity, treatment-emergent analysis flags. Useful testing dictionary-provider integration cross-domain validation. 55 variables shipped documented using labels bundled adam_spec.

what: Stamp column and dataset attributes from a herald_spec — apply_spec
title: Stamp column and dataset attributes from a herald_spec — apply_spec
text: Pre-validation helper copies CDISC metadata herald_spec onto dataset's columns onto data frame , downstream rule operators can read attributes uniformly regardless data ingested. Spec values overwrite existing attribute; columns spec row untouched. Sets attr(ds, "label") spec$ds_spec$label. Sets per-column "label", "format.sas", "sas.length", "xpt_type" spec$var_spec. Leaves datasets spec unchanged. Call validate() datasets come CSV, plain data.frames, source carry CDISC metadata. XPT Dataset-JSON readers already set attributes ingest, apply_spec() optional .

text: as_herald_spec() simple two-arg form. richer herald_spec() constructor accepts full Define-XML 2.1 slot set (study, codelist, methods, comments, ARM displays, etc.). Common upstream sources: Two raw data frames (constructor). herald_define object read_define_xml() – pass d$ds_spec d$var_spec directly. existing herald_spec – returned unchanged is_herald_spec() guards callers.

title: Construct a herald_spec object — as_herald_spec
code: dm   <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "dm.rds",   package = "herald")) adsl <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "adsl.rds", package = "herald")) adae <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "adae.rds", package = "herald"))  # ---- Dataset-only spec (no var_spec) -- sufficient for class-scoped rules ---- spec_ds_only <- as_herald_spec(   ds_spec = data.frame(dataset = "DM", stringsAsFactors = FALSE) ) is_herald_spec(spec_ds_only) #> [1] TRUE spec_ds_only$ds_spec #>   dataset #> 1      DM  # ---- Single dataset with variable list ------------------------------- spec_single <- as_herald_spec(   ds_spec  = data.frame(dataset = "DM", label = "Demographics",                         stringsAsFactors = FALSE),   var_spec = data.frame(dataset = "DM", variable = names(dm),                         stringsAsFactors = FALSE) ) nrow(spec_single$var_spec) #> [1] 25  # ---- Multi-dataset with class + label (ADaM) ------------------------- spec_adam <- as_herald_spec(   ds_spec = data.frame(     dataset = c("ADSL", "ADAE"),     class   = c("SUBJECT LEVEL ANALYSIS DATASET", "OCCDS"),     label   = c("Subject-Level Analysis Dataset", "Adverse Events"),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ),   var_spec = data.frame(     dataset  = c(rep("ADSL", ncol(adsl)), rep("ADAE", ncol(adae))),     variable = c(names(adsl), names(adae)),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) ) nrow(spec_adam$ds_spec) #> [1] 2  # ---- Rich var_spec with type, label, format, length ------------------ spec_rich <- as_herald_spec(   ds_spec  = data.frame(dataset = "DM", stringsAsFactors = FALSE),   var_spec = data.frame(     dataset  = c("DM", "DM"),     variable = c("STUDYID", "USUBJID"),     label    = c("Study Identifier", "Unique Subject Identifier"),     type     = c("text", "text"),     length   = c(12L, 40L),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) ) spec_rich$var_spec[, c("variable", "label", "length")] #>   variable                     label length #> 1  STUDYID          Study Identifier     12 #> 2  USUBJID Unique Subject Identifier     40

Formats inferred file extensions input output; pass / override. nine directions supported, including -format round-trips (useful attribute sanity checks):

text: input Path input dataset file. output Path output dataset file. One "xpt", "json", "parquet". Default: inferred tools::file_ext(output). One "xpt", "json", "parquet". Default: inferred tools::file_ext(input). dataset Dataset name override. Default: "dataset_name" attribute input data, falling back uppercased file stem input. label Dataset label override. Default: "label" attribute input data. version XPT version (5L 8L). used == "xpt"; ignored otherwise.

text: output (path written file) invisibly.

title: Convert a dataset between XPT, Dataset-JSON, and Parquet — convert_dataset
code: dm   <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "dm.rds", package = "herald")) spec <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "sdtm-spec.rds", package = "herald")) dm   <- apply_spec(dm, spec)  xpt  <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") json <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") on.exit(unlink(c(xpt, json))) write_xpt(dm, xpt)  # ---- XPT -> Dataset-JSON (format inferred from file extensions) ------ convert_dataset(xpt, json) attr(read_json(json), "dataset_name") #> [1] "DM"  # ---- Dataset-JSON -> XPT (reverse direction) ------------------------- xpt2 <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") on.exit(unlink(xpt2), add = TRUE) convert_dataset(json, xpt2) attr(read_xpt(xpt2), "dataset_name") #> [1] "DM"  # ---- Explicit format= override (no extension inference needed) ------- json2 <- tempfile()           # no extension on.exit(unlink(json2), add = TRUE) convert_dataset(xpt, json2, from = "xpt", to = "json")  # ---- Override dataset name and label at conversion time -------------- json3 <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") on.exit(unlink(json3), add = TRUE) convert_dataset(xpt, json3, dataset = "DM", label = "Demographics") attr(read_json(json3), "label") #> [1] "Demographics"

what: Detect the ADaM dataset class from column names — detect_adam_class
title: Detect the ADaM dataset class from column names — detect_adam_class
text: Infers ADaM dataset class variables present dataset spec. Uses variable signatures rather dataset name conventions works across companies name ADaM datasets differently. Classes returned: ADSL Subject-level: one row per subject, PARAMCD/AVAL. BDS Basic Data Structure: PARAMCD + AVAL present. Includes lab, vitals, ECG, exposure, similar parameter-based datasets. TTE Time--event: BDS signature plus CNSR (censoring indicator). OCCDS Occurrence Data Structure: occurrence-based without numeric AVAL parameter spine; e.g. AE, CM, MH, CE. unknown Insufficient variables determine class.

title: Detect the ADaM dataset class from column names — detect_adam_class
code: detect_adam_class(vars)

title: Detect the ADaM dataset class from column names — detect_adam_class
text: vars Character vector variable names (uppercase). Can column names data frame variable column spec.

title: Detect the ADaM dataset class from column names — detect_adam_class
text: single character string: one "ADSL", "BDS", "TTE", "OCCDS", "unknown".

title: Detect the ADaM dataset class from column names — detect_adam_class
text: Signature rules (evaluated order): TTE: PARAMCD + AVAL + CNSR present. BDS: PARAMCD + AVAL present (without CNSR). ADSL: USUBJID present, PARAMCD, AVAL, occurrence-flag pattern. OCCDS: USUBJID present + either () term variable (*TERM, *DECOD, *DOSE) (b) least two occurrence flag variables matching *FL PARAMCD. unknown: none .

title: Detect the ADaM dataset class from column names — detect_adam_class
code: adsl <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "adsl.rds", package = "herald")) advs <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "advs.rds", package = "herald")) adae <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "adae.rds", package = "herald"))  # ---- Infer class from column names of existing data frames ----------- detect_adam_class(names(adsl))  # "ADSL" #> [1] "OCCDS" detect_adam_class(names(advs))  # "BDS" #> [1] "BDS" detect_adam_class(names(adae))  # "OCCDS" #> [1] "OCCDS"  # ---- TTE class requires PARAMCD + AVAL + CNSR ------------------------ detect_adam_class(c("USUBJID", "PARAMCD", "AVAL", "CNSR", "EVDTM")) #> [1] "TTE"  # ---- Explicit character vector (e.g. from a spec variable list) ------ detect_adam_class(c("USUBJID", "SAFFL", "ITTFL", "TRTP", "AGE"))  # "ADSL" #> [1] "ADSL" detect_adam_class(c("USUBJID", "PARAMCD", "AVAL", "AVISITN"))    # "BDS" #> [1] "BDS"  # ---- Unknown when no identifying variables are present --------------- detect_adam_class(c("X", "Y", "Z"))  # "unknown" #> [1] "unknown"

title: Pilot DM (Demographics) domain — dm
text: 50-subject Demographics (DM) domain CDISC SDTM pilot submission. Contains exactly one row per subject screening / enrolment dates, baseline characteristics, treatment arm assignment, country enrolment. DM SDTM "spine": every clinical-data domain joins back DM via STUDYID/USUBJID. Useful demonstrating SDTM validation, apply_spec() metadata stamping, cross-domain rule checking. 25 variables shipped listed labels drawn bundled sdtm_spec.

title: Pilot DM (Demographics) domain — dm
code: dm

title: Pilot DM (Demographics) domain — dm
text: data frame 50 rows 25 columns. Required (mandatory) variables per SDTM-IG v2.0 flagged . STUDYID Study Identifier (text, len 12, required) – unique study code ("CDISCPILOT01" rows). DOMAIN Domain Abbreviation (text, len 2, required) – constant "DM". USUBJID Unique Subject Identifier (text, len 11, required) – study-unique subject ID; primary join key. SUBJID Subject Identifier Study (text, len 4, required) – subject ID within study. RFSTDTC Subject Reference Start Date/Time (ISO 8601 date, len 10) – typically date first study treatment. RFENDTC Subject Reference End Date/Time (ISO 8601 date, len 10) – typically date last study treatment. RFXSTDTC Date/Time First Study Treatment (ISO 8601 datetime, len 20). RFXENDTC Date/Time Last Study Treatment (ISO 8601 datetime, len 20). RFICDTC Date/Time Informed Consent (ISO 8601 datetime, len 20). RFPENDTC Date/Time End Participation (ISO 8601 datetime, len 20). DTHDTC Date/Time Death (ISO 8601 datetime, len 20) – NA surviving subjects. DTHFL Subject Death Flag (text, len 1, codelist CL.Y_BLANK) – "Y" subject died, blank otherwise. SITEID Study Site Identifier (text, len 3, required). AGE Age (integer, len 8) – numeric age consent AGEU units. AGEU Age Units (text, len 6, codelist CL.AGEU) – typically "YEARS". SEX Sex (text, len 1, required, codelist CL.SEX) – "M" "F". RACE Race (text, len 78, codelist CL.RACE) – e.g. "WHITE", "BLACK AFRICAN AMERICAN". ETHNIC Ethnicity (text, len 25, codelist CL.ETHNIC). ARMCD Planned Arm Code (text, len 8, required, codelist CL.ARMCD) – short code planned arm. ARM Description Planned Arm (text, len 20, required, codelist CL.ARM). ACTARMCD Actual Arm Code (text, len 8, required, codelist CL.ARMCD) – actual arm received; may differ ARMCD protocol violators. ACTARM Description Actual Arm (text, len 20, required, codelist CL.ARM). COUNTRY Country (text, len 3, required, codelist CL.COUNTRY) – ISO 3166 alpha-3 code (e.g. "USA"). DMDTC Date/Time Collection (ISO 8601 date, len 10) – date demographic data collected. DMDY Study Day Collection (integer, len 8) – study day DMDTC relative RFSTDTC.

what: herald: CDISC Conformance Validation for Clinical Trial Submissions — herald-package
title: herald: CDISC Conformance Validation for Clinical Trial Submissions — herald-package
text: herald pure-R conformance validator CDISC clinical trial submissions. built sponsor statistical programmers, data standards leads, regulatory submission teams preparing data packages FDA, PMDA, EMA want reproducible, scriptable alternative Java-based validators. herald reads SDTM ADaM datasets XPT, Dataset-JSON, Parquet, applies Define-XML 2.1 specification, runs published CDISC Library rule corpus data – JVM, external service, full provenance back CDISC source every finding.

title: herald: CDISC Conformance Validation for Clinical Trial Submissions — herald-package
text: herald organized seven-layer stack. layer small, stable surface; downstream layers consume output upstream layers without reaching across. Key entry points layer: L0 ingest: read_xpt(), read_json(), read_parquet(), read_define_xml() L1 spec: as_herald_spec(), herald_spec(), validate_spec() L2 stamp: apply_spec() L3 dictionaries: ct_provider(), register_dictionary(), unregister_dictionary() L4 catalog: rule_catalog(), supported_standards() L5 engine: validate() L6 report: report(), write_report_html(), write_report_xlsx(), write_report_json() See vignette("architecture", package = "herald") full design rationale.
code: 6  write_report_html() / xlsx / json     reporting  5  validate(files = ..., rules = ...)    conformance engine  4  rule corpus (YAML -> rules.rds)       rule catalog  3  Dictionary Provider Protocol          CT / SRS / MedDRA / user  2  apply_spec(datasets, spec)            pre-validation attr stamp  1  as_herald_spec() / ds_spec + var_spec specification object  0  read_xpt / read_json / read_define_xml dataset + spec ingest

title: Construct a dictionary-provider object — new_dict_provider

title: Construct a dictionary-provider object — new_dict_provider
code: new_dict_provider(   name,   version = NA_character_,   source = "unknown",   license = "unknown",   license_note = "",   size_rows = NA_integer_,   fields = character(),   contains,   info = NULL,   lookup = NULL )

title: Construct a dictionary-provider object — new_dict_provider
text: name, version, source, license, license_note, size_rows, fields Metadata fields. contains Function (value, field, ignore_case) -> logical. info Function () -> list. Defaults closure returning metadata fields . lookup Optional function (value, field) -> list.

title: Construct a dictionary-provider object — new_dict_provider
text: object class c("herald_dict_provider", "list").

title: Construct a dictionary-provider object — new_dict_provider
text: Every herald_dict_provider honours contract rule ops need know whether data comes CDISC CT, FDA SRS, MedDRA, -Drug, LOINC, SNOMED, sponsor-private table: provider$contains(value, field, ignore_case) returns logical vector aligned value. Required. provider$lookup(value, field) returns matching rows (row-bindable shape). Optional; ops need richer metadata degrade gracefully absent. provider$info() returns metadata list (name, version, source, license, license_note, size_rows, fields).

title: Construct a dictionary-provider object — new_dict_provider
text: Providers built -disk distributions (CT, SRS, MedDRA, ...) keep parsed table memory inside closure – re-parse cost across validate() calls. Bundled CDISC CT mmap-friendly loads lazily load_ct().

title: Construct a dictionary-provider object — new_dict_provider
code: # ---- Minimal provider: name + contains function ---------------------- p1 <- new_dict_provider(   name     = "my-codes",   contains = function(value, field = "code", ignore_case = FALSE) {     value %in% c("A", "B", "C")   },   fields   = "code",   size_rows = 3L ) p1$contains(c("A", "D"))        # TRUE FALSE #> [1]  TRUE FALSE p1$info()$name                   # "my-codes" #> [1] "my-codes"  # ---- With version, source, and license metadata ---------------------- p2 <- new_dict_provider(   name     = "sponsor-sex",   version  = "2026-01",   source   = "sponsor",   license  = "sponsor-private",   contains = function(value, field = "code", ignore_case = FALSE) {     value %in% c("M", "F", "U")   },   fields    = "code",   size_rows = 3L ) p2$info()$version #> [1] "2026-01"  # ---- With optional lookup function (returns matching rows) ----------- ref <- data.frame(   code  = c("M", "F"),   label = c("Male", "Female"),   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) p3 <- new_dict_provider(   name     = "sex-codes",   contains = function(value, field = "code", ignore_case = FALSE) {     value %in% ref$code   },   lookup   = function(value, field = "code") {     ref[ref$code %in% value, , drop = FALSE]   },   fields    = "code",   size_rows = nrow(ref) ) p3$lookup("M") #>   code label #> 1    M  Male  # ---- Register and use in validate() ---------------------------------- register_dictionary("sex-codes", p3) list_dictionaries() #> # A tibble: 1 × 5 #>   name      version source  license size_rows #>   <chr>     <chr>   <chr>   <chr>       <int> #> 1 sex-codes NA      unknown unknown         2 unregister_dictionary("sex-codes")

title: Read a Define-XML 2.1 file — read_define_xml
text: Parses Define-XML 2.1 file returns herald_define object carrying datasets, variables, codelists, methods, comments, ARM metadata, sponsor-defined key variables, LOINC dictionary version declared MetaDataVersion. Requires xml2 package.

title: Read a Define-XML 2.1 file — read_define_xml
code: read_define_xml(path, call = rlang::caller_env())

title: Read a Define-XML 2.1 file — read_define_xml
text: path Path Define-XML file. call Caller environment error reporting.

title: Read a Define-XML 2.1 file — read_define_xml
text: herald_define S3 object (inherits list).

title: Read a Define-XML 2.1 file — read_define_xml
text: herald round-trips following Define-XML 2.1 elements read_define_xml() -> herald_spec() -> write_define_xml(): ODM / Study / MetaDataVersion – study slot, including StudyName, ProtocolName, def:Standards. ItemGroupDef / def:Class – per-dataset metadata ds_spec, including SDTM domain, ADaM class, repeating flag, purpose, key variables. ItemDef / ItemRef – per-variable metadata var_spec, including type, length, label, ordering, mandatory flag, codelist ref, method ref. def:ValueListDef / value-level conditions – value-level slot. CodeList / EnumeratedItem / def:CodeListItem – codelist slot coded/decoded pairs extended values. MethodDef, def:CommentDef, def:leaf – methods, comments, document slots. arm:AnalysisDisplay / arm:AnalysisResult – ARM displays results slots present. Elements outside set promoted -memory object preserved verbatim possible write_define_xml().

title: Read a Define-XML 2.1 file — read_define_xml
code: tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".xml") on.exit(unlink(tmp)) writeLines(c(   '<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>',   '<ODM xmlns="http://www.cdisc.org/ns/odm/v1.3"',   '     xmlns:def="http://www.cdisc.org/ns/def/v2.1">',   '  <Study OID="S.TEST">',   '    <GlobalVariables>',   '      <StudyName>PILOT01</StudyName>',   '    </GlobalVariables>',   '    <MetaDataVersion OID="MDV.1" Name="MDV1">',   '      <ItemGroupDef OID="IG.DM" Name="DM" Repeating="No">',   '        <Description>',   '          <TranslatedText>Demographics</TranslatedText>',   '        </Description>',   '      </ItemGroupDef>',   '    </MetaDataVersion>',   '  </Study>',   '</ODM>' ), tmp)  # ---- Parse and inspect dataset metadata ------------------------------ d <- read_define_xml(tmp) d$ds_spec           # data frame: dataset, label #>   dataset        label #> 1      DM Demographics d$var_spec          # data frame: dataset, variable, label, data_type, ... #>  [1] dataset   variable  label     data_type length    origin    #>  [7] codelist  mandatory order     format    #> <0 rows> (or 0-length row.names)  # ---- Inspect study-level metadata ------------------------------------ d$study             # attribute/value pairs (StudyName, etc.) #>   attribute   value #> 1 StudyName PILOT01  # ---- Convert to herald_spec for apply_spec / write_define_xml round-trips ---- spec <- as_herald_spec(d$ds_spec, d$var_spec) is_herald_spec(spec) #> [1] TRUE  # ---- Pass directly to validate() for Define-XML dependent rules ------ r <- validate(   files  = list(DM = data.frame(STUDYID = "X", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)),   define = d,   quiet  = TRUE ) r$datasets_checked #> [1] "DM"                      "Define_Dataset_Metadata" #> [3] "Define_Study_Metadata"

code: dm   <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "dm.rds", package = "herald")) spec <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "sdtm-spec.rds", package = "herald")) dm   <- apply_spec(dm, spec) file <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") on.exit(unlink(file)) write_json(dm, file, label = "Demographics")  # ---- Read back and inspect dataset-level attributes ------------------ dm2 <- read_json(file) attr(dm2, "label")          # "Demographics" #> [1] "Demographics" attr(dm2, "dataset_name")   # "DM" #> [1] "DM"  # ---- Inspect column-level attributes preserved from write_json ------- attr(dm2$USUBJID, "label") #> [1] "Unique Subject Identifier" attr(dm2$STUDYID, "sas.length") #> [1] 12  # ---- Read then apply_spec to overwrite/supplement attributes from spec ---- dm3 <- read_json(file) |> apply_spec(spec) attr(dm3$USUBJID, "label") #> [1] "Unique Subject Identifier"  # ---- Read then validate immediately ---------------------------------- r <- validate(files = dm2, quiet = TRUE) r$datasets_checked #> [1] "DM2"

what: Read a SAS Transport (XPT) file into a data frame — read_xpt
title: Read a SAS Transport (XPT) file into a data frame — read_xpt
text: Reads V5 (FDA standard) V8 (extended) XPT transport files R data frames. Pure R implementation – SAS, haven, Java dependency. Preserves dataset variable labels, SAS formats, SAS lengths, missing-value semantics result round-trips write_xpt() without metadata loss.

title: Read a SAS Transport (XPT) file into a data frame — read_xpt
code: read_xpt(file, col_select = NULL, n_max = Inf, encoding = "wlatin1")

title: Read a SAS Transport (XPT) file into a data frame — read_xpt
text: file File path .xpt file. col_select Character vector column names read. NULL (default) reads columns. n_max Maximum number rows read. Inf (default) reads rows. encoding Character encoding XPT file. Defaults "wlatin1" (SAS WLATIN1 = Windows-1252), standard encoding SAS Windows superset 7-bit ASCII. Accepts SAS encoding names ("wlatin1", "latin1", "utf-8", "shift-jis"), aliases ("wlt1", "sjis"), standard names ("WINDOWS-1252", "ISO-8859-1"). Set NULL pass bytes without conversion.

title: Read a SAS Transport (XPT) file into a data frame — read_xpt
text: data frame single-member files (attr(df, "label"), attr(df, "dataset_name"), per-column "label", "format.sas", "sas.length", "xpt_type" attributes populated XPT header), named list data frames multi-member files.

title: Read a SAS Transport (XPT) file into a data frame — read_xpt
text: Numeric columns SAS date datetime format automatically converted R Date POSIXct classes using SAS epoch (1960-01-01). conversion based format.sas attribute stored XPT file header (NAMESTR record). Date formats (e.g. DATE9., MMDDYY10., YYMMDD10., E8601DA.) produce R Date values. Datetime formats (e.g. DATETIME20., E8601DT., DATEAMPM.) produce R POSIXct values UTC. format.sas attribute preserved converted columns round-trip fidelity write_xpt().

title: Read a SAS Transport (XPT) file into a data frame — read_xpt
text: Numeric SAS missing values (., .-.Z, ._) read NA_real_. date/datetime columns become NA dates. Character blanks (spaces) read NA_character_.

title: Read a SAS Transport (XPT) file into a data frame — read_xpt
text: Column labels stored "label" attribute column. SAS formats stored "format.sas" attribute column. dataset label stored "label" attribute data frame.

title: Read a SAS Transport (XPT) file into a data frame — read_xpt
text: XPT files contain encoding metadata. SAS Windows defaults WLATIN1 (Windows-1252), extended ASCII encoding superset 7-bit ASCII. default, read_xpt() converts WLATIN1 bytes UTF-8. -op pure ASCII files (bytes < 0x80 identical) correctly handles extended characters commonly found clinical data. Supported SAS encoding names: WLATIN1 extended ASCII characters commonly found clinical data: See full WLATIN1 map https://www.unicode.org/Public/MAPPINGS/VENDORS/MICSFT/WINDOWS/CP1252.TXT.

title: Read a SAS Transport (XPT) file into a data frame — read_xpt
text: SAS V5 transport format specification: https://documentation.sas.com/doc/en/pgmsascdc/9.4_3.5/movefile/n0167z9rttw8dyn15z1qqe8eiwzf.htm SAS V8 transport format specification: https://documentation.sas.com/doc/en/pgmsascdc/9.4_3.5/movefile/p0ld1i106e1xm7n16eefi7qgj8m9.htm Full WLATIN1 (Windows-1252) character map: https://www.unicode.org/Public/MAPPINGS/VENDORS/MICSFT/WINDOWS/CP1252.TXT IANA character set registry: https://www.iana.org/assignments/character-sets/character-sets.xhtml

title: Read a SAS Transport (XPT) file into a data frame — read_xpt
code: dm  <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "dm.rds", package = "herald")) spec <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "sdtm-spec.rds", package = "herald")) dm  <- apply_spec(dm, spec) tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") on.exit(unlink(tmp)) write_xpt(dm, tmp)  # ---- Full read -- all columns and all rows --------------------------- dm2 <- read_xpt(tmp) attr(dm2, "label") #> [1] "Demographics" attr(dm2, "dataset_name") #> [1] "DM" attr(dm2$USUBJID, "label") #> [1] "Unique Subject Identifier"  # ---- Select specific columns only (faster for large files) ----------- dm3 <- read_xpt(tmp, col_select = c("STUDYID", "USUBJID", "AGE")) names(dm3) #> [1] "STUDYID" "USUBJID" "AGE"      # ---- Read only the first 10 rows (useful for previewing) ------------- dm4 <- read_xpt(tmp, n_max = 10) nrow(dm4) #> [1] 10  # ---- Override character encoding (e.g. Latin-1 encoded legacy files) ---- dm5 <- read_xpt(tmp, encoding = "latin1") attr(dm5$USUBJID, "label") #> [1] "Unique Subject Identifier"

what: Validate CDISC clinical data against the conformance rule catalog — validate
title: Validate CDISC clinical data against the conformance rule catalog — validate
text: primary entry point CDISC conformance checking. Runs SDTM-IG, ADaM-IG, SEND-IG rules compiled catalog set clinical datasets returns herald_result carrying every finding, full rule catalog snapshot, dataset metadata. Supply datasets directory path (XPT Dataset-JSON files disk) directly named list data frames. validate() function need round-trip submission check: Loads compiled rule catalog (inst/rules/rules.rds inst/rules/spec_rules.rds) first call caches . Resolves rule scope per dataset using autodetected profile ("sdtm", "adam", "send") class hints spec. Walks rule's compiled check_tree relevant datasets, recording fired advisory findings. Returns herald_result ready report() friends. best results, stamp CDISC attributes first apply_spec().

title: Validate CDISC clinical data against the conformance rule catalog — validate
code: validate(   path = NULL,   files = NULL,   spec = NULL,   rules = NULL,   authorities = NULL,   standards = NULL,   dictionaries = NULL,   study_metadata = NULL,   define = NULL,   severity_map = NULL,   quiet = FALSE )

title: Validate CDISC clinical data against the conformance rule catalog — validate
text: path Directory path containing .xpt .json datasets. Mutually exclusive files. files single data frame list data frames. passed directly (e.g. files = dm), dataset name inferred variable name uppercased (dm -> "DM"). Explicit names required variable name match domain (e.g. list(DM = dm_loaded)). Mutually exclusive path. spec Optional herald_spec as_herald_spec() read_define_xml(). Used class resolution anchor variables. rules Character vector rule IDs run (e.g. c("CG0001", "ADaM-005")). NULL (default) runs full catalog. authorities Character vector authorities include (e.g. c("CDISC", "FDA")). NULL (default) includes . standards Character vector standards include (e.g. c("SDTM-IG", "ADaM-IG")). NULL (default) includes . dictionaries Named list herald_dict_provider objects ct_provider(), srs_provider(), meddra_provider(), etc. Per-run overrides session registry set register_dictionary(). study_metadata Named list sponsor-supplied study characteristics. Recognised key: collected_domains – character vector CDISC domain codes collected study (e.g. c("MB", "PC")). Rules require key return NA advisory absent. define herald_define object read_define_xml(). Activates Define-XML dependent rules (e.g. CG0019, CG0400) otherwise return NA advisory. severity_map Named character vector (named list domain-scoped overrides) remapping rule severities run time. Match priority (first wins): Exact rule ID: c("CG0085" = "Reject"). Regex rule ID: c("^ADaM-7[0-9]{2}$" = "High"). Severity category: c("Medium" = "High"). domain-scoped overrides use named list value: list("CG0085" = list(ADSL = "Reject", BDS = "High", default = "Medium")). Findings include severity_override column override applied. quiet Logical. Suppress progress output. Default FALSE.

title: Validate CDISC clinical data against the conformance rule catalog — validate
text: herald_result S3 object fields: findings Data frame – one row per (rule, dataset, record) finding. Columns: rule_id, dataset, row, variable, value, status ("fired" "advisory"), severity, message, severity_override. rule_catalog Data frame snapshot every rule applied, id, title, authority, standard, severity, source_url, per-rule fired_n / advisory_n counts. dataset_meta Named list – one entry per dataset row/column counts, detected class, per-dataset finding tallies. datasets_checked Character vector dataset names evaluated. skipped_refs List cross-dataset references resolved (missing datasets). timestamp POSIXct validate() called. duration difftime total elapsed time. profile Character – "sdtm", "adam", "send", "unknown" – autodetected dataset names.

title: Validate CDISC clinical data against the conformance rule catalog — validate
text: entry validate() reads compiled catalog (rules + spec pre-flight rules), narrows rules, authorities, standards. Scope resolved per (rule, dataset): rule scoped SDTM EX runs datasets detected EX, rule scoped ADaM class ("BDS", "OCCDS") consults dataset's detected class (spec$ds_spec$class spec supplied).

title: Validate CDISC clinical data against the conformance rule catalog — validate
text: severity_map overrides match order (first wins): exact rule-id, regex rule-id, severity category. domain-scoped value (named list rule-id key) consulted last using dataset's class. Findings carry severity_override column override applied.

title: Validate CDISC clinical data against the conformance rule catalog — validate
text: define = read_define_xml(...) supplied, herald materialises small set "virtual" datasets Define_Dataset_Metadata, Define_Variable_Metadata, Define_Codelist_Metadata parsed XML injects datasets. Define-XML dependent rules (e.g. CG0019, CG0400) become evaluable; without define return advisory NA.

title: Validate CDISC clinical data against the conformance rule catalog — validate
text: Operators may legitimately return NA mean "answer". validate() collapses repeated advisory rows single advisory finding per (rule, dataset) report flooded missing references. unresolved references surface result$skipped_refs.

code: # \donttest{ dm   <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "dm.rds",        package = "herald")) spec <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "sdtm-spec.rds", package = "herald")) dm   <- apply_spec(dm, spec) ae   <- data.frame(STUDYID = "PILOT01", DOMAIN = "AE", USUBJID = "PILOT01-001-001",                    AETERM = "HEADACHE", AEDECOD = "Headache",                    stringsAsFactors = FALSE)  # single dataset -- name inferred from variable (dm -> "DM") r <- validate(files = dm, quiet = TRUE) r$datasets_checked #> [1] "DM" r$findings[r$findings$status == "fired", c("rule_id", "message")] #> # A tibble: 105 × 2 #>    rule_id     message                                             #>    <chr>       <chr>                                               #>  1 1           ADSL dataset does not exist                         #>  2 CG0313      STUDYID, USUBJID or POOLID, DOMAIN, and DMSEQ exist #>  3 CG0501      TM dataset present in study                         #>  4 CG0502      TM dataset present in study                         #>  5 CORE-000655 Values between ARMCD and ACTARMCD are not matching  #>  6 CORE-000655 Values between ARMCD and ACTARMCD are not matching  #>  7 CORE-000655 Values between ARMCD and ACTARMCD are not matching  #>  8 CORE-000655 Values between ARMCD and ACTARMCD are not matching  #>  9 CORE-000655 Values between ARMCD and ACTARMCD are not matching  #> 10 CORE-000655 Values between ARMCD and ACTARMCD are not matching  #> # ℹ 95 more rows  # multiple datasets r2 <- validate(files = list(DM = dm, AE = ae), quiet = TRUE) r2$datasets_checked #> [1] "DM" "AE" r2$profile   # "sdtm", "adam", or "send" #> [1] NA # }

title: Write a Define-XML 2.1 HTML rendering — write_define_html
code: dm <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "dm.rds", package = "herald"))  # ---- Minimal spec: dataset label only -------------------------------- spec1 <- as_herald_spec(   ds_spec  = data.frame(dataset = "DM", label = "Demographics",                         stringsAsFactors = FALSE),   var_spec = data.frame(dataset = "DM", variable = "STUDYID",                         stringsAsFactors = FALSE) ) out1 <- tempfile(fileext = ".html") on.exit(unlink(out1)) write_define_html(spec1, out1) file.exists(out1) #> [1] TRUE  # ---- Richer spec with variable metadata ------------------------------ spec2 <- as_herald_spec(   ds_spec  = data.frame(dataset = "DM", label = "Demographics",                         stringsAsFactors = FALSE),   var_spec = data.frame(     dataset   = rep("DM", 2),     variable  = c("STUDYID", "USUBJID"),     label     = c("Study Identifier", "Unique Subject Identifier"),     data_type = c("text", "text"),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) ) out2 <- tempfile(fileext = ".html") on.exit(unlink(out2), add = TRUE) write_define_html(spec2, out2)  # ---- Multi-dataset spec ---------------------------------------------- adsl <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "adsl.rds", package = "herald")) spec3 <- as_herald_spec(   ds_spec  = data.frame(     dataset = c("DM", "ADSL"),     label   = c("Demographics", "Subject-Level Analysis Dataset"),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ),   var_spec = data.frame(     dataset  = c(rep("DM", ncol(dm)), rep("ADSL", ncol(adsl))),     variable = c(names(dm), names(adsl)),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) ) out3 <- tempfile(fileext = ".html") on.exit(unlink(out3), add = TRUE) write_define_html(spec3, out3)  # ---- Spec built from a parsed Define-XML (read -> render as HTML) ---- xml_tmp <- tempfile(fileext = ".xml") on.exit(unlink(xml_tmp), add = TRUE) write_define_xml(spec2, xml_tmp, validate = FALSE) d <- read_define_xml(xml_tmp) spec_rt <- as_herald_spec(d$ds_spec, d$var_spec) out4 <- tempfile(fileext = ".html") on.exit(unlink(out4), add = TRUE) write_define_html(spec_rt, out4)

code: dm   <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "dm.rds", package = "herald"))  # ---- Minimal spec with one variable -- skip pre-flight validation ---- spec1 <- as_herald_spec(   ds_spec  = data.frame(dataset = "DM", label = "Demographics",                         stringsAsFactors = FALSE),   var_spec = data.frame(dataset = "DM", variable = "STUDYID",                         stringsAsFactors = FALSE) ) tmp1 <- tempfile(fileext = ".xml") on.exit(unlink(tmp1)) write_define_xml(spec1, tmp1, validate = FALSE)  # ---- With var_spec: variable labels, types, and lengths -------------- spec2 <- as_herald_spec(   ds_spec  = data.frame(dataset = "DM", label = "Demographics",                         stringsAsFactors = FALSE),   var_spec = data.frame(     dataset   = rep("DM", 2),     variable  = c("STUDYID", "USUBJID"),     label     = c("Study Identifier", "Unique Subject Identifier"),     data_type = c("text", "text"),     length    = c("12", "40"),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) ) tmp2 <- tempfile(fileext = ".xml") on.exit(unlink(tmp2), add = TRUE) write_define_xml(spec2, tmp2, validate = FALSE)  # ---- Multi-dataset spec ---------------------------------------------- adsl <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "adsl.rds", package = "herald")) spec3 <- as_herald_spec(   ds_spec  = data.frame(     dataset = c("DM", "ADSL"),     label   = c("Demographics", "Subject-Level Analysis Dataset"),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ),   var_spec = data.frame(     dataset  = c(rep("DM", ncol(dm)), rep("ADSL", ncol(adsl))),     variable = c(names(dm), names(adsl)),     stringsAsFactors = FALSE   ) ) tmp3 <- tempfile(fileext = ".xml") on.exit(unlink(tmp3), add = TRUE) write_define_xml(spec3, tmp3, validate = FALSE)  # ---- Round-trip: write then read back -------------------------------- tmp4 <- tempfile(fileext = ".xml") on.exit(unlink(tmp4), add = TRUE) write_define_xml(spec2, tmp4, validate = FALSE) d <- read_define_xml(tmp4) d$ds_spec #>   dataset        label #> 1      DM Demographics d$var_spec[, c("dataset", "variable", "label")] #>               dataset variable                     label #> IT.DM.STUDYID      DM  STUDYID          Study Identifier #> IT.DM.USUBJID      DM  USUBJID Unique Subject Identifier

code: dm   <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "dm.rds", package = "herald")) spec <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "sdtm-spec.rds", package = "herald")) dm   <- apply_spec(dm, spec) file <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") on.exit(unlink(file))  # ---- Write with label -- dataset name inferred from variable symbol (dm -> "DM") ---- write_json(dm, file, label = "Demographics") attr(read_json(file), "dataset_name")   # "DM" #> [1] "DM"  # ---- Explicit dataset and label overrides ---------------------------- file2 <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") on.exit(unlink(file2), add = TRUE) write_json(dm, file2, dataset = "DM", label = "Demographics SDTM")  # ---- With study OID and metadata reference (for define.xml linkage) ---- file3 <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") on.exit(unlink(file3), add = TRUE) write_json(dm, file3,   dataset             = "DM",   study_oid           = "STUDY.PILOT01",   metadata_version_oid = "MDV.1",   metadata_ref        = "define.xml" )  # ---- Plain data frame without prior apply_spec (attributes inferred from variable name) ---- ae <- data.frame(   STUDYID = "X", DOMAIN = "AE", USUBJID = "X-001",   stringsAsFactors = FALSE ) file4 <- tempfile(fileext = ".json") on.exit(unlink(file4), add = TRUE) write_json(ae, file4) attr(read_json(file4), "dataset_name")  # "AE" #> [1] "AE"

title: Write a data frame to a SAS Transport (XPT) file — write_xpt
text: Writes data frame (named list data frames) XPT transport file V5 (FDA submission standard) V8 (extended) format. Pure R implementation – SAS, haven, Java dependency. Round-trips dataset / variable labels, SAS formats, SAS lengths, Date / POSIXct columns. herald.sort_keys attribute set x, data sorted keys writing.

title: Write a data frame to a SAS Transport (XPT) file — write_xpt
code: write_xpt(   x,   file,   version = 5,   dataset = NULL,   label = NULL,   encoding = "wlatin1" )

title: Write a data frame to a SAS Transport (XPT) file — write_xpt
text: x data frame, named list data frames multiple members. file File path output .xpt file. version Transport format version: 5 (default, FDA standard) 8 (extended names/labels). dataset Dataset name (e.g., "DM"). Default: "dataset_name" attribute x (set read_xpt(), read_json(), apply_spec()), uppercase file stem ("sdtm/dm.xpt" -> "DM"), "DATA". V5: max 8 characters, uppercased. V8: max 32 characters. label Dataset label. Defaults attr(x, "label") "". encoding Character encoding output file. Defaults "wlatin1" (SAS WLATIN1 = Windows-1252), converts UTF-8 characters extended ASCII SAS compatibility. Accepts SAS encoding names ("wlatin1", "latin1", "utf-8", "shift-jis") standard names ("WINDOWS-1252", "ISO-8859-1"). Set NULL write bytes -without conversion.

title: Write a data frame to a SAS Transport (XPT) file — write_xpt
text: x invisibly (input data frame, file path).

title: Write a data frame to a SAS Transport (XPT) file — write_xpt
text: R Date columns converted SAS date values (days since 1960-01-01) automatically assigned format.sas = "DATE9." unless column already format.sas attribute. Similarly, POSIXct columns converted SAS datetime values (seconds since 1960-01-01 00:00:00 UTC) format.sas = "DATETIME20.". format.sas attribute written XPT NAMESTR header SAS recognizes variable date. Informats auto-set (matching SAS behaviour); set informat.sas column writing needed.

title: Write a data frame to a SAS Transport (XPT) file — write_xpt
text: Numeric NA, NaN, Inf, -Inf written SAS missing (.). NA dates datetimes also written SAS missing. Character NA values written blank strings (spaces).

title: Write a data frame to a SAS Transport (XPT) file — write_xpt
text: Variable names must 8 characters (-Z, 0-9, underscore ), character variables 200 bytes, labels 40 characters. names uppercased.

title: Write a data frame to a SAS Transport (XPT) file — write_xpt
text: Variable names 32 characters mixed case. Labels 256 characters via LABELV8/LABELV9 extension records.

title: Write a data frame to a SAS Transport (XPT) file — write_xpt
text: default, write_xpt() converts UTF-8 character data WLATIN1 (Windows-1252) writing. ensures XPT file compatible SAS sessions using default WLATIN1 encoding. pure ASCII data, conversion -op. See read_xpt() full encoding reference table.

title: Write a data frame to a SAS Transport (XPT) file — write_xpt
text: SAS V5 transport format specification: https://documentation.sas.com/doc/en/pgmsascdc/9.4_3.5/movefile/n0167z9rttw8dyn15z1qqe8eiwzf.htm SAS V8 transport format specification: https://documentation.sas.com/doc/en/pgmsascdc/9.4_3.5/movefile/p0ld1i106e1xm7n16eefi7qgj8m9.htm Full WLATIN1 (Windows-1252) character map: https://www.unicode.org/Public/MAPPINGS/VENDORS/MICSFT/WINDOWS/CP1252.TXT IANA character set registry: https://www.iana.org/assignments/character-sets/character-sets.xhtml

title: Write a data frame to a SAS Transport (XPT) file — write_xpt
code: dm   <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "dm.rds", package = "herald")) spec <- readRDS(system.file("extdata", "sdtm-spec.rds", package = "herald")) dm   <- apply_spec(dm, spec) tmp  <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") on.exit(unlink(tmp))  # ---- V5 (FDA standard) -- dataset name inferred from variable symbol (dm -> "DM") ---- write_xpt(dm, tmp) attr(read_xpt(tmp), "dataset_name")   # "DM" #> [1] "DM"  # ---- V8 (extended names up to 32 chars) ------------------------------ tmp8 <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") on.exit(unlink(tmp8), add = TRUE) write_xpt(dm, tmp8, version = 8L)  # ---- Explicit dataset name and label overrides ----------------------- tmp3 <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") on.exit(unlink(tmp3), add = TRUE) write_xpt(dm, tmp3, dataset = "DM", label = "Demographics") attr(read_xpt(tmp3), "label") #> [1] "Demographics"  # ---- Plain data frame (no prior apply_spec) -- name from file stem ---- ae <- data.frame(STUDYID = "X", USUBJID = "X-001", stringsAsFactors = FALSE) tmp_ae <- tempfile(fileext = ".xpt") on.exit(unlink(tmp_ae), add = TRUE) write_xpt(ae, tmp_ae, dataset = "AE", label = "Adverse Events") attr(read_xpt(tmp_ae), "dataset_name")  # "AE" #> [1] "AE"

title: herald 0.1.0 (development)
text: Initial clean-slate rewrite. See plan .local/plan.md. Added current-API documentation set: expanded README, pkgdown reference structure, vignettes getting started, validation/reporting, data /O, Define-XML, dictionary providers, best practices, architecture. convert_dataset() replaces six directional converters (xpt_to_json(), json_to_xpt(), xpt_to_parquet(), parquet_to_xpt(), json_to_parquet(), parquet_to_json()). Formats inferred file extensions; pass = / = override. @examples now use pharmaversesdtm pharmaverseadam pilot datasets instead hand-crafted toy data frames. Pure-R, CRAN-clean, JVM. SDTM + ADaM + Define-XML 2.1. CDISC Library API harvested rules + controlled terminology. Define-XML full roundtrip (read / mutate / write semantic preservation). Submission-ready HTML / XLSX / JSON reports.
